records.ety: [L. indicatus, p. p. of indicare to indicate; pref. in- in + dicare to proclaim; akin to dicere to say. See Diction, and cf. Indict, Indite.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Indicated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Indicating (?).]

defs: To point out; to discover; to direct to a knowledge of; to show; to make known.
quotes: That turns and turns to indicate From what point blows the weather.

defs: To show or manifest by symptoms; to point to as the proper remedies; as, great prostration of strength indicates the use of stimulants. Opposite of contraindicate.
To investigate the condition or power of, as of steam engine, by means of an indicator.
